method: Playwright-rendered scrape of clinicaltrials.gov/study/<NCT>?tab=history&a=1 for v1 (initial registration), compared against v2 API current registered primary outcomes
match_protocol: Manual side-by-side comparison; flag drift in (a) primary-outcome wording, (b) statistical framework (e.g. time-to-event vs cumulative incidence), (c) timeFrame, (d) number of distinct primary outcomes

v1_primary.measure: Number of cardiovascular deaths and heart failure events
v1_primary.description: Composite endpoint. Heart Failure events comprise first and recurrent events

current_primary.measure: Occurrence of the Composite Endpoint of Cardiovascular Death and Total (First and Recurrent) Heart Failure Events
current_primary.timeFrame: From randomization up until the end of study, with an average study duration of 32 months

current_primary.measure: Occurrence of the Composite Endpoint of Cardiovascular Death and Total (First and Recurrent) Heart Failure Events
current_primary.timeFrame: From randomization up until the end of study, with an average study duration of 32 months

trials.notes: Composite definition unchanged. Single primary measure split into 2 (event count + participant count). Average study duration shortened from prospective 42m to retrospective 32m.

v1_primary.measure: Time to the first occurrence of any of the components of the composite: CV death or hospitalization for HF or an urgent HF visit.
v1_primary.timeFrame: From randomization visit (day 0) up to approximatley 3 years

current_primary.measure: Subjects Included in the Composite Endpoint of CV Death, Hospitalization Due to Heart Failure or Urgent Visit Due to Heart Failure.

trials.notes: Most material drift in the sample: primary outcome statistic changed from time-to-first-event (Cox / Kaplan-Meier framework) to subjects-included (cumulative-incidence framework). TF reduced 23%. Composite content (CV death + HF hosp + urgent HF) unchanged.

v1_primary.measure: Composite primary endpoint - Time to first event of adjudicated CV (Cardiovascular) death or adjudicated HHF (Hospitalisation for Heart Failure) in patients with Heart Failure with reduced Ejection Fraction (HFrEF)

current_primary.measure: Time to the First Event of Adjudicated Cardiovascular (CV) Death or Adjudicated Hospitalisation for Heart Failure (HHF)
current_primary.timeFrame: From randomisation until completion of the planned treatment period, up to 1040 days.

trials.notes: Smallest drift in the sample. Statistical framework preserved (time-to-event). 1040 days = 34.2 months, modestly shorter than originally planned 38 months.

v1_primary.measure: Time to First Occurrence of Composite Endpoint of Cardiovascular (CV) Death or Heart Failure Hospitalization

current_primary.measure: Time to First Occurrence of Composite Endpoint of Cardiovascular (CV) Death or Heart Failure (HF) Hospitalization
current_primary.timeFrame: Up to approximately 33 months (through primary analysis database cutoff date of 18-June-2019)

trials.notes: Measure preserved with cosmetic 'HF' acronym addition. TF reduced 21% from 3.5y to 33m. Reflects event-driven trial finishing earlier than maximum planned.

v1_primary.measure: Measure time to cardiovascular death or first heart failure event
v1_primary.description: Evaluate the effect of omecamtiv mecarbil/AMG 423 as compared with placebo in subjects with chronic heart failure with reduced ejection fraction receiving standard of care therapy
v1_primary.timeFrame: Through study completion, up to 208 weeks

current_primary.measure: Time to Cardiovascular Death or First Heart Failure Event
current_primary.timeFrame: From randomization to up to earliest of last confirmed survival status date or analysis cut-off date (07 August 2020); the overall median duration of follow-up was 21.8 months up to a maximum of 42 months.

trials.notes: Largest TF reduction in the sample (54%). Planned 208 weeks (48m) maximum followup; actual median follow-up 21.8 months, max 42 months. The TF wording shifted from prospective ('Through study completion, up to 208 weeks') to retrospective ('overall median duration of follow-up was 21.8 months').

summary.comparison_against_v2_api_only: v2 API current-registered vs results-section comparison detected 0/22 switching events. Initial-vs-current comparison via Playwright detected 5/5 trials with material drift. The 0% rate from v2-API-only audits is a structural artefact of the API not exposing version history; the real drift rate among pivotal HF Phase 3 trials is at least 100% on time-frame compression and 20% on statistical-framework change (this small sample).
